question: What does the then function receive in the when() method?

options.text: The current field schema that should be extended with new rules
The value of the dependency field
options.explanation: The dependency field value is passed to is (as a function). then receives the current schema for extension

options.text: An empty new schema to create from scratch
options.explanation: then receives the existing schema with already configured rules. Creating a new one loses the previous rules

options.text: An object with is, then, and otherwise fields
options.explanation: then is a callback that receives the current schema and should return the modified schema

question: Why do dependency field values come as an array in the functional syntax of when()?

options.text: Because when() can depend on multiple fields, and values are always passed uniformly as an array
It's an API design mistake
options.explanation: This is a deliberate design decision for uniformity: both single and multiple fields are passed as an array

options.text: The array is needed to support nullable fields
options.explanation: The array format is not related to nullable. It's for uniformity with one or multiple dependency fields

options.text: So that values can be mutated
options.explanation: Mutating values is not the goal. The array is used for uniform value passing

question: What are the limitations of when() in Yup?

options.text: The dependency field must be declared in the same object (shape)
Circular dependencies between fields are not allowed
when() looks for fields at the same nesting level by default
Multiple when() calls on the same field are not allowed
options.explanation: You can chain multiple when() calls on the same field — each one extends the schema

options.text: when() doesn't work with nested objects
options.explanation: when() works in nested objects but references fields at the same level

question: What happens if you create a new schema in then instead of extending the provided one?

options.text: All previously configured rules (min, max, matches, etc.) will be lost
Yup will throw an error during schema creation
options.explanation: Yup won't throw an error, but previous rules will be silently lost

options.text: The new schema will be merged with the old one
options.explanation: Yup doesn't automatically merge schemas. The schema returned from then completely replaces the current one for that condition

options.text: Nothing — both schemas will be applied
options.explanation: Only the schema returned by then is applied. Previous rules are lost

question: How does Yup handle multiple when() calls on the same field with different dependencies?

options.text: Each when() extends the schema — all conditions are applied cumulatively
Only the last when() is active
options.explanation: All when() calls are active simultaneously and complement each other

options.text: Yup picks the first matching when() and ignores the rest
options.explanation: All matching when() calls are applied, not just the first one

options.text: Multiple when() calls cause an error
options.explanation: Multiple when() calls on the same field is a valid and useful pattern
